text: Two- or one-electron-mediated difunctionalizations of internal alkenes to produce two C sp 3 stereocentres in a single operation have emerged as popular strategies in constructing complex molecules. While racemic variants of such reactions have been studied in great detail, their chiral versions, especially those involving open-shell C-centred radical species, remain scarce and often restricted to narrow ranges of applicability. In this communication, we show for the first time that enantioenriched arylsulfinylamides can serve as all-in-one reagents to access the asymmetric intermolecular aminoarylation of alkenes under mild photoredox conditions. In this mode, the nitrogen addition of the arylsulfinylamide to the alkene is coupled with a 1,4-translocation of the aromatic ring to give, in one-pot operation, aminoarylation products in high enantioselectivities. In these reactions the sulfinyl group serves as a simple traceless chiral auxiliary that is activated and simultaneously removed during the course of the mild reaction conditions applied. Under these conditions diverse β,β-diarylethylamines, aryl-α,β-ethylenediamines and α-aryl-β-aminoalcohols can be synthesized with high levels of regioselectivity, absolute and relative stereocontrol, all key structural features that are represented in numerous bioactive natural products and/or pharmaceuticals as well as in commonly used transition metal ligands.
